provenance.paper: Green WH, Ampt GA (1911) Studies on Soil Physics: Flow of air and water through soils. J Agricultural Science 4(1):1-24.
supplementary: Rawls WJ, Brakensiek DL, Miller N (1983) Green-Ampt infiltration parameters from soils data. J Hydraul Eng 109(1):62-70.
Chow VT, Maidment DR, Mays LW (1988) Applied Hydrology. Ch 5 Infiltration. McGraw-Hill.
Mein RG, Larson CL (1973) Modeling infiltration during a steady rain. Water Resources Research 9(2):384-394.
provenance.data_source: Published soil hydraulic parameters and equations (open literature)

equation.cumulative: F(t) = Ks × t + ψ × Δθ × ln(1 + F(t)/(ψ × Δθ))  [implicit]
equation.rate: f(t) = Ks × (1 + ψ × Δθ / F(t))  [from cumulative]
equation.ponding_time: tp = Ks × ψ × Δθ / (i × (i - Ks))  where i = rainfall intensity

soil_parameters.description: Green-Ampt parameters from Rawls et al. (1983) Table 1

analytical_benchmarks.name: sandy_loam_1hr
analytical_benchmarks.description: Sandy loam infiltration, initially dry (θi = 0.1), t=1hr
analytical_benchmarks.notes: Iterative solution of implicit equation; F converges in 10-20 iterations

analytical_benchmarks.name: sand_1hr
analytical_benchmarks.notes: Sand infiltrates fast; rate close to Ks from the start

analytical_benchmarks.name: clay_1hr
analytical_benchmarks.notes: Clay: high suction head dominates early, but Ks is very low

analytical_benchmarks.name: loam_ponding_time
analytical_benchmarks.description: Loam under 2 cm/hr rain intensity — when does ponding start?

analytical_benchmarks.name: silt_loam_ponding_time
analytical_benchmarks.description: Silt loam under 3 cm/hr rain — ponding time

analytical_benchmarks.name: asymptotic_rate
analytical_benchmarks.description: At large t, infiltration rate → Ks
analytical_benchmarks.notes: f/Ks should be within 5% of 1.0 at t=100hr

analytical_benchmarks.name: zero_time
analytical_benchmarks.description: At t=0, F=0 and f → infinity (theoretical)
analytical_benchmarks.notes: The rate is infinite at t=0 (soil can absorb any amount initially)

monotonicity_checks.name: cumulative_monotonic
monotonicity_checks.description: F(t) is strictly increasing with t

monotonicity_checks.name: rate_decreasing
monotonicity_checks.description: f(t) is strictly decreasing with t

monotonicity_checks.name: higher_Ks_more_infiltration
monotonicity_checks.description: Higher Ks → more F(t) at constant t

monotonicity_checks.name: higher_suction_more_early
monotonicity_checks.description: Higher ψ → more F(t) early, same Ks asymptote
